rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO CLEARING SERVICES, LLC VS. SONA RIKHYE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Hearing Re Petitioners Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs And Prejudgment Interest On Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 15, 2024, Line 1, PETITIONER WELLS FARGO CLEARING SERVICES, LLC's Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs And Prejudgment Interest On Award. Petitioner's unopposed "motion for attorneys' fees and costs and prejudgment interest on award" is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petition Of Yogurt Time, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 15, 2024, Line 10, RESPONDENTS OFFICE OF TAX APPEALS, and CALIFORNIA DEPT. OF TAX AND FEE ADMINISTRATION's Demurrer To Petition Of Yogurt Time, LLC. Respondents' demurrer to the petition is sustained without leave to amend. Petitioner violated the "pay first" rule and therefore this action fails. "No legal or equitable process shall issue in any proceeding in any court against this State or any officer thereof to prevent or enjoin the collection of any tax. After payment of a tax claimed to be illegal, an action may be maintained to recover the tax paid, with interest, in such manner as may be provided by the Legislature." (Cal. Const. Article XIII, section 32 (emphasis added).) "[T]he sole legal avenue for resolving tax disputes is a postpayment refund action." (State Bd. of Equalization v. Superior Court (1985) 39 Cal.3d 633, 638 (emphasis added).) Here, the court takes judicial notice of the Kazemini declaration and it shows that petitioner only paid the tax after filing this action. (Kazemini Decl., Ex. D.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
